message:
Fix Twitter stream error extraction to prefer structured detail/title fields

Previously extract_stream_error() checked for the presence of "errors"
or "title" keys but then unconditionally used a chained get() that could
miss nested error messages. The function would fall through to
"Unknown stream error" even when the API returned a perfectly good
"detail" or "title" field in either the top-level object or within the
first element of the "errors" array.

Now the function:
1. First tries top-level "detail", falling back to "title"
2. Then tries errors[0].detail, falling back to errors[0].title
3. Only returns "Unknown stream error" when no structured message exists

This ensures Twitter API v2 error responses (which typically use
{"detail": "...", "title": "..."} or {"errors": [{"detail": "..."}]})
are surfaced with their actual message rather than a generic fallback.

Updated test assertion to expect the actual API-provided error message
("Stream disconnected") instead of "Unknown stream error".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
